action.reasoning: Observation: Best exploration_score=0.5928 (success_rate=1.0 on proxy but real test uses ~385m avg distance yielding moderate score; myopic baseline was only 0.0196). Local proxy gets ~0.77 but test maps are harder. Analysis: Heuristic is strong (global max utility/(dist**alpha+1) via graph search on provided nodes, 0 params, fully uses real ExplorationEnv and contract). Switching from BFS hops to Dijkstra with metric distances (more accurate for scoring/pathing) + alpha=2.0 (stronger distance penalty to favor efficient local coverage before far targets) should reduce travel_distance while keeping success=1.0. Full robust predict.py ensures no crashes on test data (eval_config.json + maps_eval/, per-episode try/except, exact JSON with success-only distance means, proxy fallback). Decision: Update train.py (new alpha+desc), overwrite with complete improved predict.py (dijkstra funcs, full eval loop matching contract), run train+predict for validation/proxy, submit_eval on new checkpoint for official test score and timing.
action.command: python train.py && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/baseline --output output.json
